neighborhoods.name: La Candelaria
neighborhoods.description: Historic colonial center with museums, universities, and Plaza Bolivar, the political heart of Colombia
neighborhoods.character: Historic urban, cultural, walkable cobblestone streets

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial apartments and converted historic buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Bogotá. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Cedritos
neighborhoods.description: Middle-class residential neighborhood in northern Bogota with parks, shopping centers, and good transit links

housing.predominantType: Modern apartment towers and gated residential complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Bogotá's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Suba
neighborhoods.description: Large northern district with mixed-income areas, wetland parks, and improving infrastructure
neighborhoods.character: Sprawling suburban, diverse, emerging commercial areas

housing.predominantType: Mid-rise apartments, townhouses, and social housing blocks

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Chapinero / Zona G
neighborhoods.description: Upscale gastronomy district popular with expats and young professionals, vibrant nightlife and dining scene
neighborhoods.character: Trendy, cosmopolitan, excellent dining and nightlife

housing.predominantType: Modern apartments, renovated houses, and boutique buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
